channel.title: U.S. Police Shooting Research Tracker
channel.description: Recent academic research on police shootings and use of deadly force

item.title: Criminalizing homelessness: Longitudinal associations of police encounters and homeless sweeps with psychosocial health among the unhoused community in Los Angeles
item.description:
Authors: Chien et al.
Journal: Social Science & Medicine

item.title: Striking or grappling? Comparing public and officers' perceptions of police use of force
item.description:
Authors: Eleuterio-da-Rocha et al.
Journal: Journal of Criminal Justice

item.title: “Breaking down the barriers”: evaluating practitioners' training and practice with neurodivergent people in police custody
item.description:
Authors: Bagnall et al.
Journal: Policing: An International Journal

Purpose Neurodivergent (ND) people are reportedly overrepresented in the Criminal Justice System, but police often lack resources such as relevant knowledge and training to work with them. Furthermore, little is known about police custody practitioners' views of factors which impact their working practice with ND detained persons. ...

item.title: Protecting Children With More Guns?: The Effect of School Shootings on Parents' Gun Regulation Attitudes
item.description:
Authors: Yoo, Seungjoon
Journal: Social Science Quarterly

ABSTRACT Objectives The study examines the impact of parenthood on policy preferences regarding gun regulation in the United States. Moving beyond the conventional practice that limits parenthood to those with children under 18 residing in the same household, the study adopts a broader conception of parenthood by including all children regardless of their age. In particu...

item.title: Correction: Officer-involved shootings: testing the effect of question timing on memory accuracy for stressful events
item.description:
Authors: Porter et al.
Journal: Journal of Experimental Criminology
